common_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
Review the rule documentation for 'missing_required_column' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Remove retired stops from the export, or add them back to the trips that should serve them.
Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
Review the rule documentation for 'invalid_currency_amount' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
Check the trip updates endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
Check the flagged stop times for transposed minutes.
Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
Check the vehicle positions endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
Review the rule documentation for 'fare_transfer_rule_without_transfer_count' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Review the rule documentation for 'missing_required_field' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Review the rule documentation for 'foreign_key_violation' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_same_coordinates' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
Review the rule documentation for 'same_stop_and_route_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Review the rule documentation for 'missing_feed_contact_email_and_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
